title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

title: Iran strikes Gulf neighbors after new US attacks
Lindsey Graham, Republican hawk who became staunch Trump loyalist, has died
Pauline Vilain-Carlotti, geographer: 'The intensity of wildfires, which seems unprecedented, could become the new norm'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the environmental management specialist says wildfire risks, until now mainly limited to the Mediterranean region, are likely to increase and spread across France.

title: The rush to air-conditioned spaces: 'Full house at the cinema for 2 hours and 40 minutes of AC'
subhead: Guillemette Faure explores the growing trend in France of "AC surfing," as city dwellers move from one air-conditioned public space to another.

title: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhead: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

title: Before Emile Zola, this forgotten writer was the first to defend Alfred Dreyfus
subhead: The journalist Bernard Lazare fought relentlessly to defend Captain Dreyfus and denounce the antisemitism that drove his conviction. On Sunday, France will mark its first national day commemorating the rehabilitation of the Jewish officer.

'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
subhead: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

title: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.
byline: Lara Pino Lerro and  Romain Imbach

title: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhead: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.
